name: Injera with Doro Wot
description: Ethiopia's most beloved dish — tender slow-cooked chicken in a rich, spiced berbere sauce served on traditional injera flatbread. A must-try for all guests visiting the Amhara Region.

name: Injera with Tibs
description: Sautéed tender beef or lamb cubes with onions, tomatoes, green peppers, and rosemary, served on injera. A favorite dish throughout Ethiopia.

name: Kitfo
description: Ethiopian steak tartare — minced raw or lightly cooked beef seasoned with mitmita spice blend and spiced butter (niter kibbeh). Served with injera and ayib (Ethiopian cottage cheese).

name: Shiro Wot
description: A rich and creamy chickpea flour stew cooked with berbere spice and spiced butter, served with injera. A beloved Ethiopian fasting dish that is both nutritious and delicious.

name: Misir Wot
description: Spiced red lentil stew slow-cooked with berbere, onions, and clarified butter. A staple Ethiopian fasting dish full of protein and flavor.

name: Gomen Besiga
description: Tender collard greens cooked with beef, onions, garlic, and Ethiopian spices. A hearty and nutritious traditional dish from the Amhara region.

name: Asa Tibs
description: Fresh fish from the highland lakes, pan-fried with onions, green peppers, and Ethiopian spices. Served with injera and a side salad.

name: Mixed Vegetarian Fasting Platter
description: A generous platter of traditional Ethiopian fasting dishes including shiro, misir, gomen (collard greens), tikil gomen (cabbage), and fosolia (green beans), all served on injera.

name: Grilled Whole Chicken
description: A whole free-range chicken marinated in Ethiopian spices, slow-grilled to perfection. Served with injera, salad, and awaze sauce.

name: Pasta with Meat Sauce
description: Italian-style pasta with a rich Ethiopian-spiced meat sauce. A popular choice for international guests.

name: Ethiopian Full Breakfast
description: A hearty Ethiopian breakfast featuring firfir (spiced injera crumbles), eggs, ful (fava beans), Ethiopian honey bread, fresh fruit, and Ethiopian coffee or tea.

name: Continental Breakfast
description: A classic continental breakfast with fresh bread, butter, jam, boiled eggs, fresh fruit salad, yogurt, and your choice of coffee or juice.

name: Firfir with Eggs
description: Traditional Ethiopian breakfast of leftover injera torn and cooked with berbere sauce and clarified butter, served with scrambled or fried eggs.

name: Ful Medames
description: Mashed fava beans cooked with spiced butter, onions, and chili. Served with bread and a boiled egg — a popular Ethiopian morning staple.

name: Ethiopian Coffee Ceremony
description: The traditional Ethiopian coffee ceremony — freshly roasted Yirgacheffe or Sidama beans ground and brewed in a clay jebena pot, served in three rounds (abol, tona, baraka) with popcorn or kolo.

name: Macchiato
description: Ethiopia's beloved coffee drink — a perfect balance of strong espresso and steamed milk foam. Served in the authentic Ethiopian style.

name: Tej — Ethiopian Honey Wine
description: Traditional Ethiopian honey wine (mead) brewed with gesho (buckthorn). A sweet, fermented beverage enjoyed at celebrations and special occasions. Served chilled in a traditional berele glass.

name: Fresh Avocado Juice
description: Thick and creamy fresh avocado juice blended with a touch of honey and lime. A popular Ethiopian street drink brought to the hotel.

name: Fresh Mango Juice
description: Freshly squeezed Ethiopian mango juice — thick, sweet, and naturally delicious. Served chilled.

name: Tella — Ethiopian Local Beer
description: Traditional Ethiopian home-brewed beer made from teff, barley, or sorghum with gesho for bitterness. A mild and refreshing traditional beverage.

name: Soft Drinks
description: Selection of Coca-Cola, Pepsi, Sprite, Fanta, or water. Served chilled.

name: Sambusa
description: Crispy fried pastry triangles filled with spiced minced meat or lentils — a popular Ethiopian street food snack. Served with awaze dipping sauce.

name: Vegetarian Sambusa
description: Crispy fried pastry triangles filled with spiced lentils and vegetables. Perfect for fasting days.

name: Ayib Fresh Cheese
description: Ethiopian cottage cheese served with mitmita spice and a drizzle of niter kibbeh. A light and refreshing starter.

name: Kolo
description: Traditional Ethiopian roasted grain mix with barley, chickpeas, and sunflower seeds. A popular healthy snack served with drinks.

name: Baklava
description: Layers of flaky pastry filled with chopped nuts and sweetened with honey syrup. A delicious dessert popular across Ethiopia.

name: Ethiopian Honey Cake
description: A moist and delicious cake made with Ethiopian highland honey, spices, and teff flour. Served warm with a side of cream.

name: Fresh Fruit Platter
description: A generous platter of seasonal Ethiopian fresh fruits including mango, papaya, banana, avocado, and guava.
